chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI Criminal Bail Application No.379 of 2025 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATUREs OF JUDGEs 1. For orders on M.A. No.2237/2025 (U/A) 2. For orders on M.A. No.2238/2025 (Exp/A) 3. For hearing of bail application ------------------------- 11.02.2025 Mr. Irshad Ali Bhatti, advocate for applicant ------------------------------------ Applicant/accused Syed Asghar Ali Shah son of Mehdi Shah seeks protective bail in FIR No.78/2025, registered at P.S. Gadap City for offence under sections 354, 392, 427, 457, 506-B, 337-A(i), 34, PPC. Learned counsel for the applicant is innocent and has been falsely implicated in this case in order to humiliate and disgrace him; that he is a law abiding citizen and intends to surrender before the trial Court but he apprehends his arrest at the hands of the police for mala fide reasons. Without touching the merits of the case, protective bail is granted to applicant/accused Syed Asghar Ali Shah son of Mehdi Shah, for a period of four days from today, subject to furnishing solvent surety in the sum of Rs.25,000/- (Rupees twenty five thousand) and P.R bond in the like amount to the satisfaction of the Nazir of this Court. Applicant/accused is directed to surrender before the trial Court in the aforesaid period and join the trial forthwith. This order shall cease to have its effect after expiry of four days from today or whenever applicant/accused surrenders before the trial Court, which one occurs earlier. It is made clear, in case, applicant/accused fails to surrender before the trial Court in the aforesaid period, his bail bond shall stands automatically forfeited.

This order shall cease to have its effect after expiry of four days from today or whenever applicant/accused surrenders before the trial Court, which one occurs earlier. It is made clear, in case, applicant/accused fails to surrender before the trial Court in the aforesaid period, his bail bond shall stands automatically forfeited. The instant criminal bail application is accordingly disposed of. J U D G E Gulsher/PS
